Petition To Authorize Sale Of Securities [Probate Code 10200]
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Melvin Neal for and with James Rothman.

The Court noted that posting has occurred. Mr. Neal stated that the current bond in place is in the amount of $10,000.00. The Court ordered that bond be increased by $207,000.00. The Court granted the Petition to Authorize Sale of Securities (subject to review of the order). Status on filing of the additional bond was set for September 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Authorize Sale Of Securities [Probate Code 10200]
Off Calendar Aug-19-2014 Continued To Aug-26-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Melvin Neal for and with James Rothman.

The Court noted that the Probate Examiner raised the issue of Probate Code 10200 which requires that notice of sale of securities should be posted. Mr. Neal stated the sale proceeds for the bar in the amount of $217,000.00 could be placed in a blocked account. The Amended Petition to Authorize Sale of Securities was continued to August 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to determine if bond will be posted or if there will be a blocked account. The Administrator is to post notice of sale of securities. The Court will shorten time for posting to 5-days.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chin. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Humphrey appeared by Court Call for James Rothman who was present in court. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Mr. Ferdon stated that they did not receive notice, that they only received the Probate Examiner's notes, that he is appearing today to waive notice, and that he is also appearing to notify the Court that the Public Administrator will decline. The Court addressed the amount of the bond. Mr. Humphrey stated that the only asset is a fifty percent interest in a California Corporation - Zecky's Bar, that the lease is coming up for renewal, that Mr. Rothman has been running the bar, and that Mr. Rothman will be filing a creditor's claim. In addition, Mr. Humphrey stated that there were liens placed on the bar by Mr. Yalcin, that there may be IRS or Franchise Tax liens as well as other 3rd party claims, that they don't know the amounts at this time, and that they will need to notice all known creditors. Mr. Humphrey stated that clearly Mr. Yalcin was not a resident of Turkey that he returned there to die. Mr. Rothman stated that Mr. Yalcin's wife shared the same address as Mr. Yalcin until he died and then she moved. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $10,000.00. Discussions were held with regard to Mr. Rothman continuing to run the business and his authority to do so re: the fifty percent interest in the bar. Mr. Humphrey indicated that he would check into it noting that Mr. Rothman is fifty percent owner of the bar, that he has been managing the bar, and thatMr. Rothman had legal authority prior to the death of Mr. Yalcin.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Benton. (Not Reported)
